title: pinus pseudostrobus var. oaxacana
intro: Pinus pseudostrobus var. oaxacana, also known as the Oaxacan white pine, is a variant of the Pinus pseudostrobus tree species native to Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of compounds such as terpenes and flavonoids. The Oaxacan white pine is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion or decoction of its needles and cones, with potential applications in traditional medicine and modern herbalism.
